summary: Legendary ’87 show with scorching Bertha and tight jams, captured on a patched FM‑matrix that’s good despite some frequency quirks.
review: The performance is hailed as one of the best of the hot summer ’87 tour – highlights include an all‑rounder Sugaree, a blistering Ramble On Rose, a legendary Bertha and a soaring Dear Mr. Fantasy. Recording wise the composite FM source (matrix) has been nicely patched but suffers from uneven highs/lows and a clipped tail on Lovelight, so it’s listenable but not pristine.

ai_rating.rationale: The reviewer gives five stars and praises both the musical performance and the recording (noting only minor EQ/clipping issues), leading to a high overall rating despite limited review count.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Composite FM broadcast patched with other sources; frequencies are uneven (need roll‑off around 600 Hz and boost >8 kHz); last seconds of Lovelight are clipped.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds solidly with bright tempos; second set kicks off with an uptempo Playin' and maintains high energy through Terrapin, a strong Drums/Space segment, and finishes on a party‑closing Lovelight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jer’s guitar is described as "ON" throughout, especially on Ramble On Rose.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil goes off during Greatest Story Ever Told and shines on Playing in the Band; both drummers and Phil are praised for their energy.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s rhythm work is highlighted on My Brother Esau and Throwing Stones, where he drives the band overdrive.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) plays crazily, matching the level on Dear Mr. Fantasy.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers (Bill & Mickey) amaze; Drums segment is called "good".
